source_pr.title: test: merge serial tests in ddl, infoschema, session, store, table, telemetry and types
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Problem Summary:

After #30692, all the tests are run in serial. The names of *_serial_test.go are no longer appropriate.

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: - t.Skip("exhaustive types test, skip race test") | tk.MustExec("update t set col_2 = 47 where col_1 in ('aaaaa') order by col_1,col_2;") (session/clustered_index_test.go)
failure_signature: - t.Skip("exhaustive types test, skip race test") | tk.MustExec("update t set col_2 = 47 where col_1 in ('aaaaa') order by col_1,col_2;") (session/clustered_index_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: - t.Skip("exhaustive types test, skip race test") | tk.MustExec("update t set col_2 = 47 where col_1 in ('aaaaa') order by col_1,col_2;") (session/clustered_index_test.go).
fix_pattern: - t.Skip("exhaustive types test, skip race test") | tk.MustExec("update t set col_2 = 47 where col_1 in ('aaaaa') order by col_1,col_2;") (session/clustered_index_test.go)

evidence: evidence: - t.Skip("exhaustive types test, skip race test") | tk.MustExec("update t set col_2 = 47 where col_1 in ('aaaaa') order by col_1,col_2;") (session/clustered_index_test.go)
